critical_issues: Page count summary table (Section 3.10) has an arithmetic error in the Settings row: the inventory enumerates 9 MVP settings pages (#25-#31, #33-#34) but the summary lists only 8 MVP. This makes the total MVP page count 34 (not 33) and the grand total 39 (not 38). The error propagates to the cross-reference manifest (page_count_mvp: 33, page_count_total: 38) and will cause Phase 7 to undercount build scope by one page.
contradictions: Settings Index page #25 description states 'TM/SC see Profile + Notifications only' and decision D55-P6B-023 says 'Lucia sees only Profile and Notifications,' but Notification Preferences (#32) is a Growth-tier page. Under the feature gating principle D55-P6B-005 ('Growth features hidden entirely'), TM/SC would only see Profile under Settings at MVP launch, not Profile + Notifications.

Create Referral Partner page #19 restricts Role Visibility to 'Operator, Senior Concierge' but D2 Section 4.2 permission matrix grants Team Members create/update access (📝) to referral partner records. Note: D5's API spec (POST /api/referral-partners restricted to Operator, SC) has this same conflict with D2, so 6B inherits a pre-existing D2-vs-D5 contradiction.

Navigation sidebar item counts are inconsistently described: Notifications (header bell icon) is included in the Operator total ('8 items = 7 primary + Settings') but excluded from the TM/SC total ('6 items = 5 primary + Settings'), despite both groups having Notifications access per the Section 2.2 role-conditional table. TM/SC count should be 7 (6 primary including Notifications + Settings) if Notifications is counted, or Operator count should be 7 (6 primary + Settings) if it is not.
warnings: Booking Detail page #6 Feature ID list omits F-046 (Template Customization Workflow) and F-049 (Post-Trip Follow-Up Sequence) even though Appendix B's D3 reconciliation table explicitly maps both features to that page's tabs. The page inventory should include all feature IDs it implements for Phase 7 traceability.

Client Payments page #22 references 'payment status aggregation' as an API endpoint, but D5/D6 defines no dedicated payment aggregation or dashboard endpoint. A new endpoint may need to be added to D6 or the page must aggregate data from existing booking-level payment endpoints.

The cross-reference manifest page list is incomplete — it omits Auth Callback (#2), all 5 system pages (#35-#39), and 3 Growth pages (Vendor List #20, Vendor Detail #21, Commission Invoice Detail #24), reducing its value as a quick Phase 7 reference.

Section 6 (Notification Design System) provides detailed toast notification specifications described as applicable at launch (e.g., Section 6.5 'At launch, Tier 1 notifications also generate an email via Resend'), but D3 classifies both F-052 (Critical Push Notifications) and F-053 (Routine In-Platform Notifications) as Growth-tier features. The boundary between what notification UI exists at MVP versus Growth is unclear and could cause Phase 7 scoping confusion.

The property/course hierarchy from D4 is abstracted away in the UI — courses are the primary navigation entity with no dedicated property page. While this is a reasonable UX decision, it should be explicitly noted for Phase 7 developers who will need to understand that course profile pages fetch property-level data (commission terms, contacts) via the property_id relationship.
